mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window with Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack reduces corporate wage pressures and signals economic deceleration, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >65% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, which forces earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline MoM
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 20-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in industrial production signals immediate supply chain bottlenecks and inventory drawdowns, causing short-term market repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not precede negative 5-day returns during post-shock recovery phases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below chance (50%) in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation runs above 3% while the federal funds rate remains below 2%, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting equity market repricing toward growth assets and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combination of high CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS fails to produce positive 60-day returns, with a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all tested regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 1.0%, corporate profit margins contract due to weak demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.0% fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with forward returns remaining positive in >60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in unemployment indicates a broad-based economic contraction, triggering immediate risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and rising UNRATE does not predict negative 5-day returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 51% over a 3-year period.
